note: I don't know your normalize_image_bytes() exact implementation/signature semantics (tuple[bytes,bytes] is ambiguous to me - mimetype is a str not bytes). Please send back a DRAFT with the full proposed body of normalize_image_bytes() AND the merged image_to_base64() that also calls self.get_image_mimetype() (my new method) for mimetype resolution instead of inline filetype.guess(). Here is my starting proposal for image_to_base64 body (pre-merge, ignoring your helper): 

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        if not self.image:
            return self
        self.get_image_mimetype()
        self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
        return self

Let's converge on one merged version that includes both normalize_image_bytes() and get_image_mimetype().

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: get_image_mimetype() + image_to_base64() validator (normalize_image_bytes unchanged from your draft)
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is guaranteed to be base64-encoded bytes, and
    decoded_for_guessing holds the raw bytes suitable for mimetype guessing.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.

        If image_mimetype is already set, the cached value is returned
        directly. Otherwise the mimetype is guessed from the image bytes
        using the filetype library and the result is cached on the instance
        for future accesses.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        guess = filetype.guess(base64.b64decode(self.image))
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)

        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self
note: My task spec explicitly requires a no-arg lazy CACHING accessor get_image_mimetype() callable externally at any time (not just from image_to_base64) that checks self.image_mimetype first and caches into it. Your version takes decoded_image as a param and doesn't cache - that doesn't satisfy my spec. Proposal: keep your normalize_image_bytes() exactly as drafted, but make get_image_mimetype() no-arg and self-contained (it base64-decodes self.image itself since by the time it runs self.image is already base64-encoded). image_to_base64 calls normalize_image_bytes then self.get_image_mimetype(), discarding the decoded_for_guessing tuple member. Full merged code:

def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is guaranteed to be base64-encoded bytes, and
    decoded_for_guessing holds the raw bytes suitable for mimetype guessing.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image's mimetype.

        If image_mimetype is already set, the cached value is returned
        directly. Otherwise the mimetype is guessed from the image bytes
        using the filetype library and the result is cached on the instance
        for future accesses.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        guess = filetype.guess(base64.b64decode(self.image))
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)

        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self
fields.note: My task spec explicitly requires a no-arg lazy CACHING accessor get_image_mimetype() callable externally at any time (not just from image_to_base64) that checks self.image_mimetype first and caches into it. Your version takes decoded_image as a param and doesn't cache - that doesn't satisfy my spec. Proposal: keep your normalize_image_bytes() exactly as drafted, but make get_image_mimetype() no-arg and self-contained (it base64-decodes self.image itself since by the time it runs self.image is already base64-encoded). image_to_base64 calls normalize_image_bytes then self.get_image_mimetype(), discarding the decoded_for_guessing tuple member. code: def normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) where
    encoded_image is guaranteed to be base64-encoded bytes, and
    decoded_for_guessing holds the raw bytes suitable for mimetype guessing.
    """
    try:
        decoded_for_guessing = base64.b64decode(image, validate=True)
        encoded_image = image
    except binascii.Error:
        decoded_for_guessing = image
        encoded_image = base64.b64encode(image)

    return encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily guess and cache the mimetype of the stored image bytes."""
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        _, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self
